llm_verdict.message:
PLAN: To remediate the finding "Ensure the Default C Shell Umask is Set Correctly" (accounts_umask_etc_csh_cshrc), I will:
1. Check if the C shell is installed using rpm -q csh
2. Check if /etc/csh.cshrc exists
3. Create a backup of /etc/csh.cshrc before making changes
4. Use sed to ensure the umask is set to 077, handling cases where:
   - The line is commented out (e.g., '# umask 077')
   - The line exists but with a different value
   - Multiple umask lines exist (remove all and add one correct line)
5. Verify the umask is set correctly after remediation

The approach will follow the STIG remediation pattern while addressing the feedback about backups and handling multiple umask lines.
